title: Understand: weathering and erosion
description: Check your understanding of weathering and erosion in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

assessment_items.question_content:
Sea caves typically form as a result of weathering and erosion by ocean waves. When waves strike a coastline, they break pieces of softer or weaker rock away from the land. These pieces are then swept away by the waves, leaving behind a fissure in the rock.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which type of weathering forms sea caves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.caption: *Sea caves in Cape Greco National Forest Park, Cyprus*

assessment_items.question_content:
Marble buildings are vulnerable to weathering by acid rain. When this rainwater falls, the acid in the water can react with the stone and dissolve it, causing damage to the structure.

**Which type of weathering is this an example of?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Karst is a type of landscape formed by the weathering of limestone. Karst landscapes are created when acid rain reacts with limestone and dissolves the rock. Over time, this results in distinctive landforms, such as the towers shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which type of weathering forms karst landscapes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

**Which is an example of weathering by water?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Rainwater dislodges soil particles from the ground.
Ocean waves drop sand onto a beach.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is an example of erosion by water?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A river transports sand and silt downstream.
Ocean waves deposit sediment onto a beach.
Rainwater reacts with minerals in a rock.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is an example of deposition by water?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ocean waves drop sand onto a beach.
A stream dislodges sediment from its banks.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How can humans reduce erosion along a riverbank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: by planting trees and bushes on the riverbank
by removing all of the vegetation from the riverbank
by redirecting water into the river to increase its volume

assessment_items.question_content:
**How can humans reduce erosion along a riverbank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: by increasing the amount of vegetation on the riverbank
by cutting down all of the trees on the riverbank
by breaking up the soil on the riverbank into smaller pieces

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which action is most likely to reduce erosion along a streambank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: planting grasses, shrubs, and other plants on the streambank
clearing all of the vegetation away from the streambank
breaking up the soil on the streambank into smaller pieces

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which two conditions generally increase the erosional power of a river?**
